title: thelypteris boydiae
intro: Thelypteris boydiae, also known as the boyd's maiden fern, is a type of flowering plant in the fern family (Thelypteridaceae) native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds, making it beneficial for wound healing and skin irritations. The health benefits of Thelypteris boydiae include reducing inflammation and promoting tissue repair, as well as acting as a natural antimicrobial agent to prevent infections. Herbal preparations of Thelypteris boydiae can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using a tincture to extract its medicinal compounds.
